key_insight: Detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings oxidize slowly at 450°C and evolve into a stronger multiphase structure that helps preserve protection over time.
engineering_relevance: The coating offers a promising thermal-barrier option for TC4 titanium alloys exposed to long-term high-temperature service.
method: Fabricated AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings by detonation spraying.
Aged/oxidized the coatings in air at 450°C for up to 250 h.
Tracked oxidation kinetics, phase evolution, and microhardness changes over time.
evidence_signal: The oxidation followed a parabolic law with kp = 1.83 × 10^-2 mg^2·cm^-4·h^-1, while microhardness increased from 491 HV0.2 to 665.2 HV0.2 after 250 h.
watch_item: The beneficial hardening comes with phase decomposition of the primary quasicrystal, so coating performance may depend on controlling Al depletion and phase-boundary stability.
paper_title: High-temperature oxidation kinetics and microstructural evolution of detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings
